skill.name: compress
skill.description: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
skill.summary: Compress markdown files to caveman format, saving 65-75% of tokens while preserving all code, URLs, and structure.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced 53 potential findings, all evaluated as false positives. The subprocess.run call to 'claude' CLI uses hardcoded arguments with no user input. Environment variable access (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CAVEMAN_MODEL) is expected functionality for an AI tool. File path references to .env, .aws, .ssh are part of a security denylist that prevents compressing sensitive files. No confirmed security risks found.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution with Fallback
medium_findings.description: The skill uses subprocess.run to execute the 'claude' CLI command as a fallback when the Anthropic Python SDK is unavailable. Arguments are hardcoded with no user input, eliminating command injection risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of subprocess.run call, but command is hardcoded ['claude', '--print'] with no user-controlled input - legitimate fallback pattern for AI tooling

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and CAVEMAN_MODEL environment variables to make API calls. This is expected functionality for an AI tool that compresses files by sending them to Claude.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear pattern of environment variable access for API key and model configuration - legitimate use case for AI tool that requires Claude API access

low_findings.title: File Overwrite with Backup Protection
low_findings.description: The skill overwrites the original file with compressed content after creating a backup at FILE.original.md. Checks for existing backup to prevent data loss.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File modification pattern with backup protection - minimal risk as backup is created before overwrite and existing backups are checked

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Path Denylist (Protective Pattern)
dangerous_patterns.description: The code implements a denylist of sensitive file patterns (.env, .ssh, .aws, credentials, keys, etc.) and refuses to compress files matching these patterns. This is a security feature, not a vulnerability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Clear implementation of protective denylist - SENSITIVE_BASENAME_REGEX and SENSITIVE_PATH_COMPONENTS are used to refuse processing sensitive files

content.user_title: Compress Memory Files to Save Tokens
content.value_statement: AI memory files grow large and waste input tokens. This skill compresses natural language prose to caveman format, saving 65-75% of tokens while preserving all code, URLs, and technical content.
actual_capabilities: Compress markdown files to caveman format reducing token usage by 65-75%
Preserve all code blocks, URLs, file paths, and technical terms exactly
Validate compressed output to ensure no content was lost or modified
Create automatic backups as FILE.original.md before overwriting
Refuse to compress sensitive files like credentials, keys, and .env files
Retry up to 2 times with targeted fixes if validation fails
limitations: Only works with natural language files (.md, .txt, markdown, rst)
Requires Python 3.10+ and either Claude CLI or Anthropic API key
Maximum file size limit of 500KB to prevent excessive API usage
Sends file contents to Anthropic API for compression
Cannot compress files with extensions like .py, .js, .json, .yaml, .env

use_cases.title: Compress Large CLAUDE.md Files
use_cases.description: When your CLAUDE.md project memory file grows beyond 5000 tokens, compress it to caveman format to save input tokens on every Claude session while preserving all technical details.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude Code with large project memory files

use_cases.title: Optimize Todo and Preference Files
use_cases.description: Compress personal productivity files like TODO.md, preferences.md, or notes.md that contain natural language instructions, reducing token usage without losing action items or settings.
use_cases.target_user: Users who maintain markdown-based todo lists or preference files

use_cases.title: Prepare Documentation for AI Context
use_cases.description: Before pasting documentation into Claude for analysis, compress verbose explanations to caveman format to fit more content within the context window while keeping all technical specifications intact.
use_cases.target_user: AI power users who work with large documentation files

prompt_templates.title: Basic File Compression
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress my CLAUDE.md file to caveman format
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple one-command compression for your project memory file

prompt_templates.title: Compress Specific File
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress the file at /path/to/notes.md using caveman format
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to compress a specific file by providing its path

prompt_templates.title: Compress Multiple Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress all markdown files in my /docs directory to caveman format
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch compression of documentation files to reduce token usage

prompt_templates.title: Verify Compression
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress README.md and show me the token savings comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to see how much you saved after compression

output_examples.input: You should always make sure to run the test suite before pushing any changes to the main branch. This is important because it helps catch bugs early and prevents broken builds from being deployed to production.
output_examples.output: Run tests before push to main. Catch bugs early, prevent broken prod deploys.

output_examples.input: The application uses a microservices architecture with the following components. The API gateway handles all incoming requests and routes them to the appropriate service. The authentication service is responsible for managing user sessions and JWT tokens.
output_examples.output: Microservices architecture. API gateway route all requests to services. Auth service manage user sessions + JWT tokens.

best_practices: Always review the .original.md backup before deleting it to ensure compression quality
Test compression on non-critical files first to verify the output meets your needs
Keep original backups of files containing complex code blocks until you validate the compression preserved them exactly
Use the compression trigger /caveman:compress <filepath> for precise file targeting
anti_patterns: Do not compress code files like .py, .js, .json, or .yaml as the skill will refuse them
Do not compress files containing sensitive credentials, API keys, or passwords
Do not delete the .original.md backup file until you have verified the compressed version works correctly
Do not use this skill on binary files or files larger than 500KB

faq.question: What files can I compress?
faq.answer: Only natural language files with extensions like .md, .txt, .markdown, or .rst. Code files such as .py, .js, .json, .yaml, and .env are automatically skipped to preserve functionality.

faq.question: Is my original file safe?
faq.answer: Yes. A backup is created at FILE.original.md before any modification. If compression fails after retries, the original file is automatically restored. The skill also refuses to proceed if a backup already exists.

faq.question: Does compression change code blocks or URLs?
faq.answer: No. Code blocks, inline code, URLs, file paths, commands, and technical terms are preserved exactly. Only natural language prose is compressed to caveman format.

faq.question: What happens if compression fails?
faq.answer: The skill validates the output and retries up to 2 times with targeted fixes. If validation still fails after retries, the original file is restored and the backup is deleted.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: The skill first tries the Anthropic Python SDK with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. If unavailable, it falls back to the Claude CLI which handles its own authentication. Set CAVEMAN_MODEL to override the default claude-sonnet-4-5.

faq.question: Can I compress files with credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill refuses to compress files matching patterns like .env, credentials, secrets, .ssh, .aws, .gnupg, and other sensitive paths. Compression sends content to an external API, so sensitive files are blocked as a security measure.
